name: Between the Valley and the Castle Trail
name_original: Entre o Vale e o Castelo — percurso circular (PR9 MCQ)
description: Hike the easy 3.0-mile Between the Valley and the Castle circular route (PR9 MCQ) to explore Monchique's valleys, forests, and castle ruins.
region_intro: Hiking around Alferce offers a distinct "green Algarve" experience within the Monchique Mountains, characterized by higher altitudes and lush vegetation. The region features deep valleys, crystal-clear streams, and extensive cork oak forests. This environment supports unique plant life and provides a varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Alferce The most popular hiking route is Between the Valley and the Castle Trail, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This easy route explores valleys and forests, leading to the ruins of Alferce Castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Barranco do Demo Boardwalk – Barranco do Demo Walkway loop from Alferce, an easy 2.6 miles (4.3 km) path. This trail includes sections of the recently inaugurated wooden walkway and suspension bridge over the dramatic Barranco do Demo gorge. Local hikers also love the Monchique Viewpoint – Barranco do Demo Walkway loop from Alferce, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views towards Monchique, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Alferce is defined by the Monchique Mountains, deep valleys, and extensive cork oak forests. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate trails. The routes in Alferce are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 2000 reviews. More than 9000 hikers have used komoot to explore Alferce's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alferce?
A: Alferce offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore the unique landscapes of the Monchique Mountains, from deep valleys to extensive cork oak forests.

Q: What makes hiking in Alferce unique compared to other parts of the Algarve?
A: Hiking in Alferce provides a distinct "green Algarve" experience. Unlike the coastal areas, this region is characterized by higher altitudes and humidity within the Monchique Mountains, fostering lush vegetation, crystal-clear streams, and impressive cork oak forests. It offers a tranquil escape into nature.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alferce?
A: The Monchique Mountains, where Alferce is located, generally offer pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year due to their higher altitude. Spring and autumn are ideal, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Even in winter, the climate can be suitable for hiking, though some routes might be wetter.

Q: Are there any notable natural landmarks or viewpoints to discover while hiking in Alferce?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty and panoramic views. You can explore impressive cork oak forests, dramatic features like the Barranco do Demo gorge, and breathtaking viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/735919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foia Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/111049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picota Summit&lt;/a&gt;, which offer expansive vistas across the hills and even the distant Atlantic Ocean.

Q: Can I visit the Alferce Castle ruins on a hike?
A: Absolutely. The ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421739980/between-the-valley-and-the-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between the Valley and the Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy circular route that allows you to explore the valleys and forests of Monchique, culminating in the ruins of Alferce Castle. These ruins, with origins tracing back to the Bronze Age, offer spectacular views from their hilltop perch.

Q: Is there a boardwalk or suspension bridge trail in Alferce?
A: Yes, the recently inaugurated Barranco do Demo Walkway is a highlight. This wooden walkway includes staircases and a 50-meter-long suspension bridge that dramatically spans the deep Barranco do Demo gorge. You can experience it on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19319734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco do Demo Boardwalk – Barranco do Demo Walkway loop from Alferce&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Alferce?
A: Yes, Alferce offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421739980/between-the-valley-and-the-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between the Valley and the Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19319734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco do Demo Boardwalk – Barranco do Demo Walkway loop from Alferce&lt;/a&gt; are generally considered suitable for all ages, though the boardwalk does have some sections with numerous steps.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Alferce?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 2400 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's tranquil mountainous atmosphere, the unique "green Algarve" experience, and the well-maintained trails that offer stunning panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Alferce?
A: Many of the trails around Alferce are circular, allowing you to start and end your hike in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421739980/between-the-valley-and-the-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between the Valley and the Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32201099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monchique Viewpoint – Barranco do Demo Walkway loop from Alferce&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: Are there any other towns or attractions nearby that I can combine with a hike in Alferce?
A: Yes, Alferce is well-situated for exploring other nearby attractions. You can visit the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/86636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monchique Town Center&lt;/a&gt;, known for its traditional crafts and thermal baths, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/130522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caldas de Monchique Thermal Baths&lt;/a&gt; for relaxation after your hike. The region also features impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1034710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;cork oak groves&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes around Alferce?
A: Hikes around Alferce vary in duration depending on the route and your pace. Easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19319734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco do Demo Boardwalk – Barranco do Demo Walkway loop from Alferce&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 15 minutes, while moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32187394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stream crossing in Monchique – Monchique Viewpoint loop from Alferce&lt;/a&gt; might take over 2 hours 30 minutes.
tips: Right at the beginning of the route in Alferce, there is this beautiful picnic park. It's actually a simple table with benches, but the key point is the beautiful view of the valley below.

In summer 2023, a new wooden walkway with stairs and a suspension bridge was built to make this beautiful valley and great views accessible even for hikers who are not sure-footed. 
In summer, only recommended in the morning or evening hours - down in the valley it can be calm and very hot. Please always take something to drink with you. In the northern section there are approx. 213 (possibly +13) steps, in the southern section approx. 352 steps - a total of just over 1100 there and back. Access in the north behind the cemetery (tarmac road), in the south at the starting point of this tour recording (gravel road, only a few parking spaces).

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Between the Valley and the Castle circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 4.76 km (2.96 miles) long and typically takes around 1 hour and 34 minutes to complete at a moderate pace. However, if you take your time to enjoy the scenery and explore the Picnic area with views over the Monchique Valley or the Barranco do Demo Walkway, it might take a bit longer.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as an easy hike. While it features some steep ascents and descents along dirt paths, it's generally suitable for most hikers without extensive preparation. Be aware that conditions can be muddy and slippery in winter.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the PR9 MCQ trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of dirt paths. You'll encounter sections with steep ascents and descents as it zigzags along the slopes of the Serra de Monchique. Depending on water levels, there might be a section where you need to take your shoes off to cross the Monchique stream.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is renowned for its varied and fantastic scenery. You'll experience green valleys, dense cork oak forests, and views of the excavated Vale do Demo (Demo Valley) with its waterfalls. Key highlights include the Picnic area with views over the Monchique Valley and the Barranco do Demo Walkway. From various points, especially near the castle ruins, you'll get panoramic views over the Monchique mountains and even glimpses of the sea.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other trails. It covers 93% of the 'Entre o Vale e o Castelo' trail and significant portions of the 'Passadiços Barranco do Demo' (52%). Smaller sections also overlap with 'Percurso da Fonte Santa', 'Pelos caminhos de Alferce', and parts of the 'Via Algarviana' network.

faqs.question: Is the Between the Valley and the Castle circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on this trail. However, as it passes through the Monchique Special Area of Conservation and areas with diverse birdlife, it's recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round. During warmer months, it's advisable to start early in the morning to avoid the heat. In winter, be prepared for potentially muddy and slippery conditions, especially if there has been recent rain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Between the Valley and the Castle circular route (PR9 MCQ). It is freely accessible to the public. However, as it's located within the Monchique Special Area of Conservation, please respect the natural environment and follow general trail etiquette.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through diverse habitats, including dense cork oak forests, which are home to various bird species. You might spot chaffinches, tits, woodpeckers, and warblers. The area's rich biodiversity adds to the appeal of the hike.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the PR9 MCQ trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, as a circular route, you can typically find parking near the trailhead in the Monchique municipality. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking spots.

faqs.question: What is the significance of the castle ruins mentioned along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail features the archaeological site of Cerro do Castelo de Alferce, which contains the ruins of an Islamic fortification. This castle, likely built in the 9th century AD, is one of Portugal's oldest remaining Muslim castles and is classified as a site of public interest. Its strategic hilltop location offers excellent panoramic views.

waypoints.name: Picnic area with views over the Monchique Valley
